name: Sophia (Hanson Robotics)
short_description: Hyper-realistic social humanoid robot with lifelike silicone skin, more than 60 facial expressions, and AI-driven conversation — a cultural inflection point that brought humanoid robots into mainstream media debate.
long_description: Sophia was activated on 14 February 2016 by Hong Kong–based Hanson Robotics and made her public debut at SXSW in March of that year. Built around a patented silicone skin material called Frubber, she can produce over 60 distinct facial expressions via servo-actuated muscle-like structures beneath the face. Her AI stack combines OpenCog for general reasoning, a deep-neural-network motion generator, face-tracking and emotion-recognition algorithms, and CereProc's text-to-speech synthesis. Sophia became a global cultural event in October 2017 when Saudi Arabia granted her citizenship — the first time a country extended legal personhood to a robot — and she was later named a UN Development Programme Innovation Champion. She sits at the intersection of social robotics, performance, and public discourse, demonstrating how humanoid form and real-time conversation can reshape popular expectations of AI embodiment even when the underlying AI remains narrower than it appears.
ai_note: Real AI components run inside Sophia — face tracking, emotion recognition, deep-neural-network gesture generation, and NLP via OpenCog — but her conversational depth is partially scripted and the system is best described as a hybrid of AI and theatrical presentation.

images.caption: Sophia robot at the AI for Good Global Summit, Geneva, June 2017. Photo by ITU Pictures.
images.alt_text: Sophia, a humanoid robot with realistic silicone skin, dark hair, and expressive eyes, photographed at a conference. Her neck and upper torso reveal mechanical components beneath a human-like face.

sources.title: Sophia (robot)
sources.access_notes: Comprehensive overview of activation date (14 Feb 2016), SXSW debut, Frubber skin, 60+ facial expressions, AI stack (OpenCog, CereProc, deep neural networks), Saudi citizenship event (Oct 2017), and UN Innovation Champion appointment (Nov 2017).
